summary:
A preservative solution such as 10% formalin should be used to keep the tissue sample intact for examination by the pathologist after it has been removed during surgery due to its ability to stabilize cells before processing under a microscope. Agent1 reasoned that based on common practice with other biopsies requiring long-term storage or transport, using 10% formalin preserves cell structures effectively while preventing decomposition until further analysis can occur at pathology services. 

Note: There was no need to include "thus" since this summary directly states what action needs to happen without needing an explicit conclusion statement linking back to previous information provided within one sentence instead focusing more clearly around core elements needed from patient care perspective regarding proper post-operative specimen management procedures required when dealing specifically with histopathological samples derived through surgeries including those related male reproductive organs where maintaining integrity throughout transportation process prior actual testing becomes crucial aspect ensuring accurate diagnostic outcomes later stage once received into laboratory setting equipped necessary equipment capable performing complex analyses upon these formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 1 summarized): A preservative solution such as 10% formalin should be used to keep the tissue sample intact for examination by the pathologist after it has been removed during surgery due to its ability to stabilize cells before processing under a microscope. Agent1 reasoned that based on common practice with other biopsies requiring long-term storage or transport, using 10% formalin preserves cell structures effectively while preventing decomposition until further analysis can occur at pathology services. 

Note: There was no need to include "thus" since this summary directly states what action needs to happen without needing an explicit conclusion statement linking back to previous information provided
